title: rubus × eriophyllus
intro: Rubus × eriophyllus, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, combining the characteristics of the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the dewberry (Rubus canadensis). The leaves and fruit of the black raspberry plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming black raspberry include reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Black raspberry is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also used as a flavoring agent in foods and beverages.
